get.summary: ListCollections returns all collections matching the optional search query.
get.description:
Results are sorted by collection name ascending. Pagination is controlled by the query's
pagination field with a default page size of 1000.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: CreateCollection creates a new resource collection.
post.description:
The collection name must be non-empty after whitespace trimming. At least one resource selector
or embedded collection ID must be provided. The caller's identity is recorded as the creator.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the name is empty, or if no selectors or embedded collections are provided.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateCollectionRequest is the request message for CreateCollection.

post.summary: DryRunCollection simulates creating or updating a collection without persisting it.
post.description:
Validates the collection definition and, when options.with_matches is true, resolves and
returns the deployments that would match. Set id to simulate an update to an existing
collection; leave id empty to simulate a new collection.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the collection definition is invalid (empty name, no selectors).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: DryRunCollectionRequest is the request message for DryRunCollection.

get.summary: ListCollectionSelectors returns all supported field labels that can be used in resource selectors.
get.description:
Supported selectors include fields such as "Cluster", "Namespace", and "Deployment Label".
Requires read access to the WorkflowAdministration resource.

get.summary: GetCollection returns a single collection by ID.
get.description:
When CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions.with_matches is true, also resolves and returns the
deployments currently matched by the collection's selectors, optionally further filtered
by filter_query.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no collection with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the collection to retrieve.

parameters.name: options.withMatches
parameters.description: with_matches, when true, resolves the collection's selectors and returns all deployments that match.

parameters.name: options.filterQuery.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

delete.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns a conflict error if the collection is referenced by another collection or by a
vulnerability report configuration.

patch.summary: UpdateCollection updates an existing collection's name, description, selectors, and embedded collections.
patch.description:
Replaces the full set of selectors and embedded collection references.
The caller's identity is recorded as the last updater.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty, the name is empty, or no selectors or embedded collections are provided.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the collection to update.

get.summary: GetCollectionCount returns the count of collections matching the optional query.
get.description: Matches all collections if the query is empty.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

name.description: name is the new display name for the collection. Must be non-empty after trimming whitespace.

description.description: description is the new human-readable explanation of the collection's purpose.

resourceSelectors.description: resource_selectors replaces the current set of rules that match deployments into this collection.

embeddedCollectionIds.description: embedded_collection_ids replaces the current set of embedded collection references.

CollectionServiceUpdateCollectionBody.description: UpdateCollectionRequest is the request message for UpdateCollection.

id.title: 'id' is searchable to force a separate table

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

resourceSelectors.description: `resource_selectors` resolve as disjunction (OR) with each-other and with selectors from `embedded_collections`. For MVP, the size of resource_selectors will at most be 1 from UX standpoint.

fieldName.title:
`field_name` can be one of the following:
- Cluster
- Cluster Label
- Namespace
- Namespace Label
- Namespace Annotation
- Deployment
- Deployment Label
- Deployment Annotation

values.description: `values` resolve as a conjunction (AND) or disjunction (OR) depending on operator. For MVP, only OR is supported from UX standpoint.

withMatches.description: with_matches, when true, resolves the collection's selectors and returns all deployments that match.

filterQuery.description:
filter_query is an optional search query to further filter the matched deployments.
Uses StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production"). Only used when with_matches is true.

v1CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions.description: CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions controls whether the response includes matching deployments.

name.description: name is the display name for the new collection. Must be non-empty after trimming whitespace.

description.description: description is an optional human-readable explanation of the collection's purpose.

resourceSelectors.description:
resource_selectors define the rules that match deployments into this collection.
At least one resource selector or embedded collection ID must be provided.

embeddedCollectionIds.description:
embedded_collection_ids are IDs of existing collections whose members are included in this collection.
At least one resource selector or embedded collection ID must be provided.

v1CreateCollectionRequest.description: CreateCollectionRequest is the request message for CreateCollection.

collection.description: collection is the newly created resource collection with its assigned ID.

v1CreateCollectionResponse.description: CreateCollectionResponse is the response message for CreateCollection.

name.description: name is the display name of the collection being dry-run.

id.description:
id is the UUID of an existing collection; set when dry-running an update to an existing collection.
Leave empty when simulating a new collection.

set if dryrun on existing collections

description.description: description is the description for the collection being dry-run.

resourceSelectors.description: resource_selectors define the rules to evaluate for this dry run.

embeddedCollectionIds.description: embedded_collection_ids are IDs of collections to include in this dry run.

options.description: options controls whether matching deployments are returned in the response.

v1DryRunCollectionRequest.description: DryRunCollectionRequest is the request message for DryRunCollection.

deployments.description:
deployments contains deployments that would match the collection without persisting it.
Only populated when CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions.with_matches is true.

v1DryRunCollectionResponse.description: DryRunCollectionResponse is the response message for DryRunCollection.

count.description: count is the number of collections matching the query.

v1GetCollectionCountResponse.description: GetCollectionCountResponse is the response message for GetCollectionCount.

collection.description: collection is the requested resource collection.

deployments.description:
deployments contains deployments matching the collection's selectors, populated only when
CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions.with_matches is true.

v1GetCollectionResponse.description: GetCollectionResponse is the response message for GetCollection.

selectors.description: selectors is the list of supported field selector labels (e.g. "Cluster", "Namespace", "Deployment Label").

v1ListCollectionSelectorsResponse.description: ListCollectionSelectorsResponse is the response message for ListCollectionSelectors.

collections.description: collections is the list of resource collections matching the query, sorted by name ascending.

v1ListCollectionsResponse.description: ListCollectionsResponse is the response message for ListCollections.

sortOptions.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

query.title:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

pagination.description: pagination controls page size and sort order for list results.

v1RawQuery.description:
RawQuery represents the search query string.
The format of the query string is "<field name>:<value,value,...>+<field name>:<value, value,...>+..."
For example:
To search for deployments named "central" and "sensor" in the namespace "stackrox", the query string would be
"Deployment:central,sensor+Namespace:stackrox"
RawQuery is used in ListAPIs to search for a particular object.

aggregateBy.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

collection.description: collection is the updated resource collection.

v1UpdateCollectionResponse.description: UpdateCollectionResponse is the response message for UpdateCollection.
